units.title: My family
units.subtitle: Meet the family and learn how to talk about feelings — aligned with Cambridge Prepare Lv1 Unit 2.
units.phonics_focus: /ə/ schwa (mum, brother, son, father, mother, parents)

prepare_alignment.source: Cambridge Prepare 2nd Edition Level 1 — Unit 2 'My family' (SB p18-21)

spiral_meta.grammar_anticipation_next_unit: there is / there are (Unit 3 — passive exposure only)

lessons.title: Meet the family
lessons.topic: Family relations — mum, dad, brother, sister, baby, parents. Determiners (my, your).

steps.instruction: Look at Ruby's family photo. Answer.

questions.correct_answer: her mum, her dad and her brother
options: her mum, her dad and her brother

items.definition: Your female parent. Same as 'mother', friendly word.
Your male parent. Same as 'father', friendly word.
A boy with the same parents as you.
items.example_sentence: I have one brother, his name is Sam.

items.definition: A girl with the same parents as you.
A very young child.
items.example_sentence: Helena's baby is six months old.

items.definition: Your mother and father together.
items.example_sentence: Inca's parents are Helena and David.

games.instruction: Match each picture with the right word.
Listen to the word and tap the right picture.
Look at the picture and type the word.

steps.title: My family
steps.text: Hi! I'm Ruby. I'm 11. This is my family. My mum is from Spain — her name is Sofia. My dad is from the UK — his name is Mark. I have one brother — his name is Sam. Sam is 9. We have a baby in our family too — her name is Lily. Lily is one. My parents are kind and my brother is funny. I love my family.
steps.scene_description: A family photo on a sofa: mother, father, an 11-year-old girl, a 9-year-old boy, and a baby.

questions.question: What is the name of Ruby's brother?
Who is the baby in the family?
Ruby's dad is from the UK. True or false?

steps.explanation: We use my and your to say who something belongs to. 'My mum' = the mum of me. 'Your brother' = the brother of you. We say 'my mum is from Spain' — not 'I mum'.

steps.audio_text: Hi! I'm Mark. I'm Ruby's dad. This is my family. My wife is Sofia. We have two children — Ruby and Sam. Ruby is 11 and Sam is 9. We have a baby — her name is Lily. We are a happy family!
steps.scene_description: A father introduces his family at home.

questions.question: How many children does Mark have?

steps.prompt: Tell us about your family. Say 2-3 sentences with 'My mum...', 'My dad...', 'My brother/sister/baby...'.
steps.expected_text: My mum is from [country]. My dad is [job/from]. My brother is [age/name].
prompts: Start: 'My mum is...' or 'My dad is...'
Add one more person: 'My brother is...' or 'My sister is...'
Say one feeling: 'My family is happy/funny/kind.'

questions.question: A very young child is a ___.
Your mother and father together are your ___.
True or false: 'My brother' means the brother of me.

lessons.title: More about families
lessons.topic: Family relations — mother, father, husband, wife, son, daughter, children, family. Possessive 's.

steps.instruction: Look at David's family photo. Answer.

questions.question_text: How many daughters do David and Helena have?

items.definition: Your female parent.
items.example_sentence: Helena is the mother of three children.

items.definition: Your male parent.
A woman's male partner in marriage.
A man's female partner in marriage.
A male child of a parent.
A female child of a parent.
More than one boy or girl.
A group of people related to you.
items.example_sentence: We are a big family — six of us.

games.instruction: 60 seconds! Tap the right picture for each word.
Read the word and tap the right picture.
Flip the cards and find the pairs.

steps.title: Helena's family
steps.text: My name is Helena. I'm a mother. My husband is David. We have three children — three daughters. Their names are Isabel, Inca and Eva. Isabel is 14. Inca and Eva are twins — they are 11. We are a big family. We're happy together.
steps.scene_description: A mother stands with her husband and three daughters in a garden.

questions.question: How many daughters does Helena have?
Helena and David have a son. True or false?

steps.explanation: We add 's after a person's name (or after a noun like 'mum') to say something belongs to that person. 'David's wife' = the wife of David. 'Helena's daughter' = the daughter of Helena. For names ending in 's' we still add 's.

steps.audio_text: Hello! I'm Isabel. I'm 14. I have two sisters — Inca and Eva. They are twins. They are 11. My mother's name is Helena. My father's name is David. I'm Helena's daughter and David's daughter. We have a big family — five of us!
steps.scene_description: A teenage girl introduces her family.

questions.question: How many sisters does Isabel have?
How many people are in the family?

steps.prompt: Talk about a family you know. Use 'X's wife', 'X's daughter', 'X's son' to describe two people.
steps.expected_text: David's wife is Helena. Helena's daughter is Isabel.
prompts: Start with one person's name + 's: 'David's wife is...'
Say one more thing about them: 'She is 14.' or 'They are happy.'

questions.question: A man's female partner in marriage is his ___.
More than one boy or girl are ___.
True or false: 'Inca's sister' means the sister of Inca.

lessons.title: How do you feel?
lessons.topic: Feelings adjectives — happy, sad, tired, bored, hungry, thirsty, hot, clever, funny. be: short answers.

steps.instruction: Watch Inca and Eva. How do they feel today?

questions.question_text: Inca is smiling. How does she feel?

items.definition: Not interested. Nothing fun to do.
Quick at learning. Smart.
Makes you laugh.
You feel good. You smile.
Too warm — you feel uncomfortable.
You want to eat food.
You don't feel good. You cry.
items.example_sentence: Inca is sad — her cat is sick.

items.definition: You want to drink water.
You want to sleep or rest.

games.instruction: Listen to the word, then type it.
Listen and tap the right word.

steps.title: A long day
steps.text: It's Sunday afternoon. Helena and her three daughters are at home. Isabel is in her room. She is tired — it's a hot day. Inca is in the kitchen. She is hungry. She wants a sandwich. Eva is in the garden. She is bored — she wants to play. Helena is in the living room. She is happy — she has tea and a book. It's a long day, but a good day.
steps.scene_description: Helena and her daughters in different rooms of the house on a hot afternoon.

steps.explanation: When someone asks a 'be' question (Are you...? Is he...?), we answer with a short answer. Yes + subject + be. No + subject + be + not. We don't say just 'yes' — we say 'Yes, I am.' or 'No, I'm not.'
examples: Are you tired? — Yes, I am.
Is Inca hungry? — Yes, she is.
Is Eva sad? — No, she isn't. She's bored.
Are you happy? — No, I'm not.
Are Inca and Eva twins? — Yes, they are.

items.sentence: ___ Eva sad? — No, she isn't. She's bored.
___ Inca and Eva twins? — Yes, they are.
___ Eva sad? — No, she isn't. She's bored.
___ Inca and Eva twins? — Yes, they are.
am you tired? — Yes, I am.
am Inca hungry? — Yes, she is.
am Eva sad? — No, she isn't. She's bored.
am you happy? — No, I'm not.
am Inca and Eva twins? — Yes, they are.

steps.audio_text: MUM: Isabel! Are you OK? ISABEL: I'm tired, Mum. MUM: Are you hungry? ISABEL: No, I'm not hungry. I'm thirsty. MUM: OK. Here's some water. Is Inca in her room? ISABEL: No, she isn't. She's in the kitchen. She's hungry! MUM: Funny girl! ISABEL: Yes, she is.
steps.scene_description: A mother and daughter conversation at home.

steps.prompt: How do you feel today? Say two sentences. 'I'm ___' and 'I'm not ___'.
steps.expected_text: I'm happy today. I'm not tired.
prompts: Pick one feeling: happy, tired, hungry, thirsty, sad, bored, hot.
Now say one feeling you don't have: 'I'm not...'

questions.question: You want to drink water. You are ___.
You feel good and you smile. You are ___.
True or false: 'I'm not hot' means I don't feel hot.

lessons.title: My family — Review
lessons.topic: Recycle Unit 2 family + feelings vocab + all three grammar points. Pronunciation /ə/.

steps.instruction: Look at three families. Match each person to a feeling.

questions.question_text: Helena's baby is one. The baby is happy. True or false?
Sam is hungry. Sam is Ruby's ___.

games.instruction: Climb the ladder! Choose the right word for each step.
90 seconds! Race through all the words you learned.

steps.title: A photo on the wall
steps.text: On the wall in Ruby's room there is a photo. It's a family photo. Ruby is in the photo with her family. Her mum, Sofia, is happy. Her dad, Mark, is funny. Her brother, Sam, is clever — he's 9. Lily, the baby, is in Sofia's arms. Lily is happy too. Ruby loves the photo. It's her family.
steps.scene_description: A family photo on a bedroom wall.

questions.question: Where is the family photo?
options: on the wall in Ruby's room
questions.correct_answer: on the wall in Ruby's room

patterns: be: short answers — Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.

items.sentence: be: short answers — Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
be: short answers — Yes, I ___ / No, I'm not.
be: short answers — Yes, I ___ / No, I'm not.

steps.audio_text: Hi! My name is Eva. I'm 11. My twin sister's name is Inca. We are Helena's daughters. Our father is David. Today I'm happy — it's my birthday! Inca is hungry, but I'm not. I'm thirsty — I want water. We are a funny family.
steps.scene_description: Eva talks about her family on her birthday.

steps.prompt: Talk about your family for 30 seconds. Use family words, possessive 's, and one feeling.
steps.expected_text: My mum's name is Sofia. My dad is funny. I'm happy with my family.

questions.question: You feel good and you smile — you are ___.
True or false: 'My dad's brother' is your uncle.
